# koryph

> Multi-project orchestrator for autonomous Claude Code agents: beads-native, account-safe, subscription-first. `koryph` runs autonomous implementation waves across many git projects — reading each project's beads ready-graph, batching conflict-free work by declared footprint, dispatching headless Claude Code agents into isolated git worktrees under the correct Claude account, checkpointing to disk, governing subscription burn, and merging finished work through a green gate.

This file is a machine-readable index for AI agents and tools ingesting the project. Links point to the canonical Markdown sources on GitHub (fetchable as raw text). The same book is published at https://koryph.build/.

## Install

Prerequisites: `git`; the Claude CLI (`claude`, from https://claude.ai/download, authenticated via `claude auth login`); the `bd` (beads) CLI (https://github.com/gastownhall/beads, confirm with `bd doctor`). `gh` (GitHub CLI) is optional but simplifies the steps below.

koryph is a single static binary — no Go toolchain or runtime required to run it. Detect your platform, fetch the latest release, verify it, install it:

```sh
os="$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"     # darwin | linux
arch="$(uname -m)"; case "$arch" in x86_64) arch=amd64 ;; aarch64|arm64) arch=arm64 ;; esac

# with gh (simplest — resolves the latest release automatically)
gh release download --repo koryph/koryph -p "koryph_*_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz" -p checksums.txt

# without gh (curl + GitHub API only)
tag="$(curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/koryph/koryph/releases/latest | grep -m1 '"tag_name"' | cut -d '"' -f4)"
curl -fsSLO "https://github.com/koryph/koryph/releases/download/${tag}/koryph_${tag#v}_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz"
curl -fsSLO "https://github.com/koryph/koryph/releases/download/${tag}/checksums.txt"

shasum -a 256 -c --ignore-missing checksums.txt   # macOS; use `sha256sum -c --ignore-missing checksums.txt` on Linux
tar -xzf koryph_*_"$os"_"$arch".tar.gz koryph
install -m 0755 koryph ~/.local/bin/              # any directory on PATH
koryph version                                    # expect: koryph <version>
```

Building from source instead requires Go 1.21+: `go install github.com/koryph/koryph/cmd/koryph@latest`.

On macOS the tap is the simplest path: `brew install koryph/tap/koryph` (no Go required; the tap ships a cask, and Homebrew casks are macOS-only — on Linux use the tarball above or `go install`). Full prerequisites, PATH troubleshooting, and shell completions: [Installation](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/installation.md). To verify a download's checksum, cosign signature, and SLSA provenance before trusting it: [Supply-chain verification](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/supply-chain.md).

## Enable koryph in a project (agent runbook)

This section is a complete, executable sequence for an AI agent asked to
"enable koryph in this project". The fast path is the one-command adoption
wizard; a manual step-by-step fallback follows for anything the wizard
can't resolve non-interactively.

### Fast path: `koryph adopt`

```sh
koryph adopt <repo-root> --yes --json
```

Precondition: `koryph version` works — if the binary itself is missing,
install it first (macOS: `brew install koryph/tap/koryph`; other
platforms: the Install section above), asking the human before running any
installer. Everything else below is the wizard's job.

`koryph adopt` detects the repo, installs missing prerequisites (`claude`,
`bd`, `gh`) with consent, initialises/hardens beads, derives the account,
`gate`, `forge`, and `area_map`, installs the agent scaffolding, offers one
signed `chore: adopt koryph` commit, and finishes by running `koryph
validate` in-process — all in one run. `--yes` accepts only unambiguous
derivations and fails closed, naming the flag to set, on anything ambiguous:

- **`--account <profile> --identity <email>`** — zero or 2+ verified Claude
  account candidates found (checked against `~/.claude.json` and each
  `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`'s copy).
- **`--gate "cmd1;;cmd2"`** (repeatable, or one `;;`-separated flag) — no
  gate command could be inferred from the repo (Makefile/package.json/
  go.mod/Cargo.toml/pyproject.toml).
- **`--forge github|gitlab`** — the git remote does not match a known forge
  host.

`--json` emits `{root, project_id, steps[]}` on stdout (progress moves to
stderr); a non-zero exit means at least one step is `blocked` or `koryph
validate` did not pass — inspect `steps[].state` for what remains. Preview
the plan first with `koryph adopt <repo-root> --dry-run --json`, which
writes nothing. Re-running `adopt` on an already-adopted repo is a no-op
report — it doubles as an onboarding-scoped `koryph doctor`. Adoption also
installs a `/koryph-adopt` slash command into `.claude/commands`, so once
one repo is adopted, later agent sessions can run this whole sequence
conversationally (for this repo as a repair pass, or for another repo by
path) instead of re-deriving it from this runbook. Full phase-by-phase
contract:
[koryph adopt](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/adopt.md).

### Manual fallback (step by step)

Use this path when a fast-path `blocked` step needs something the current
environment can't resolve non-interactively (e.g. a tool install needing
`sudo`), or when each step should run independently. Execute it top to
bottom; every step is idempotent and safe to re-run. Ask the human only for
the two values in step 2 (account profile name, account email).

1. **Preconditions.** `koryph version` works (else run the Install section
   above); the target is a git repository; the agent runtime CLI is
   authenticated (e.g. `claude --version`); `bd` (beads) is installed. If the
   repo has no beads database yet, initialise it first: `bd init` in the repo
   root (confirm with `bd stats`).

2. **Register the project** (creates the machine-local registry record,
   scaffolds `koryph.project.json` if missing, and installs the agent
   scaffolding — `AGENTS.md`, `koryph-*` personas/commands, hooks/rules for
   runtimes that support them; never clobbers existing files):

   ```sh
   koryph project add <repo-root> --account <profile> --identity <email>
   ```

3. **Complete `koryph.project.json`.** Three fields matter most and cannot be
   guessed by koryph:
   - `gate`: the project's REAL build/test/lint commands — this is the merge
     gate; agents' work only lands when these pass.
   - `area_map`: directory-prefix → area-token map that powers footprint
     scheduling (parallel agents without merge conflicts). Map each top-level
     source directory to a short token (`"src/api": "api"`).
   - `forge`: the hosted git service — `github` (default) or `gitlab`. Omit
     for GitHub; set `"forge": "gitlab"` for GitLab projects. koryph sniffs the
     git remote to suggest a value but never overrides this field. See the
     Choosing-a-forge chapter for the capability table and GitLab token flow.

4. **Optional hardening** (recommended for repos that ship):
   - `koryph signing enable --project <id>` — SSH commit signing, key served
     from a vault (Proton Pass/1Password/macOS Keychain/encrypted file).
   - `koryph posture apply oss-solo-maintainer --repo <owner>/<repo>` —
     branch protection + repo security settings; diff-first, shows changes
     before applying.
   - `koryph release setup --project <id>` — the release train (versioning,
     SBOM/signature/provenance releases).

5. **Validate**: `koryph validate <id>` then `koryph doctor` — both must be
   clean (validate reaches `validated` after the first successful run).

6. **Create dispatch-shaped work.** Tasks live in beads. To be dispatched a
   bead MUST have type `task`, `bug`, or `chore` (never `epic`/`feature`) and
   SHOULD carry one `area:<token>` label per area it touches (tokens from
   `area_map`; unlabeled beads serialize behind everything). Use the
   installed `/koryph-plan` skill to decompose a design or prompt into a
   correctly-footprinted, dependency-linked graph, or create directly:
   `bd create "title" --type task --labels "area:api"`.

7. **First run** (single pass, review + auto-merge):

   ```sh
   koryph run --project <id> --once --auto-merge --review
   ```

   Watch with `koryph board` / `koryph roster --project <id>`. Merged work
   lands on the default branch only after review findings are addressed and
   the gate is green.

## Operating contract (read first)

- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): the koryph protocol every agent follows — beads-only task tracking, Conventional Commits + DCO + SSH-signed commits, protected paths, the green gate, and the `refactor-core` self-hosting rule.
- [README](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/README.md): project invariants (account-safe, subscription-first, beads-as-truth), install, and the package layout table.
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/AGENTS.md): canonical cross-runtime agent instructions.

## Concepts

- [Concepts overview](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/index.md): the ideas in dependency order — work, parallelism, time, safety, money, people, hygiene, shipping, the fence.
- [The lifecycle](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/lifecycle.md): the whole SDLC loop from prompt → design → beads → dispatch → review → gate → merge → epic validation → release, with the stage-by-stage map.
- [Beads and the ready-graph](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/beads.md): the dependency-aware issue database that feeds the scheduler.
- [Footprints](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/footprints.md): declared touch-sets that make parallel dispatch conflict-free.
- [Resources](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/resources.md): `res:<kind>` counted capacities — footprints protect the merge; resources protect the machine.
- [Rolling dispatch](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/rolling-dispatch.md): continuous slot refill vs waves.
- [Worktrees and the green gate](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/worktrees.md): isolation, protected paths, review → rebase → gate → fast-forward merge.
- [Governors and quota](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/governors.md): subscription-first spend, AIMD concurrency, quota throttling.
- [Accounts and personas](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/accounts.md): fail-closed identity, personas, model tiers.
- [Postures and vaults](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/postures.md): hygiene as named, applyable configuration; vault-served secrets.
- [The release train](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/release-train.md): draft-until-complete, signed, attested releases.
- [Ejectability](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/concepts/ejectability.md): the opinionation boundary — delete koryph and your repo is still ordinary.

## User guide

- [Installation](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/installation.md): install `koryph`; per-user machine-local registry.
- [Quickstart](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/quickstart.md): register a project and run a first wave end-to-end.
- [koryph adopt](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/adopt.md): the onboarding wizard — detect → plan → confirm → execute → verify in one command, with a non-interactive/`--json` contract for agents.
- [Zero to shipped](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/zero-to-shipped.md): the full journey across the three pillars (Build/Protect/Ship) with today's shipped commands.
- [Projects & accounts](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/projects-and-accounts.md): the `~/.koryph` registry, per-project account isolation, identity verification.
- [Choosing a forge](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forges.md): the `forge` field + remote sniffing, GitHub vs GitLab capability table, GitLab setup end-to-end (token flow, CI variables, release pipeline, supply-chain gap).
- [From prompt to beads](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/describing-work.md): intent routing — how a described ask becomes a design doc (`/koryph-design`, with a mandatory human approval stop) and then a dispatch-shaped bead graph (`/koryph-plan`).
- [Running waves](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/running-waves.md): the wave loop (scan → batch → preflight → dispatch → poll → review → merge), `--once`/`--only`/`--budget`.
- [Merge reconcilers](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/merge-reconcilers.md): self-healing merges for derived artifacts (lockfiles, generated indexes) that collide even when their inputs don't.
- [Recovery & escalation](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/recovery.md): stall/death detection, cause-coded retries, final-attempt escalation to the frontier tier, learned model labels, and the operator override toolkit (`inject`, `status --frontier`, the overrides sidecar).
- [AI runtimes: support status](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/runtimes.md): Claude Code is the production runtime; all other runtimes (codex, cursor, gemini, grok, copilot, opencode, amp) are ALPHA — dispatch to them is refused fail-closed; contributions welcome.
- [Billing & quota](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/billing-and-quota.md): subscription-first dispatch, the quota governor, throttling, calibration.
- [Context economy](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/context-economy.md): the native levers that keep agent context lean so quota goes to real work.
- [Headroom integration](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/headroom-integration.md): EXPERIMENTAL — pointing the `agent_proxy` seam at the third-party headroom-ai context-compression proxy.
- [Signing](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/signing.md): SSH commit signing via vault providers (Proton Pass, KeePassXC, 1Password, …).
- [Posture profiles](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/postures.md): named desired-state security bundles — branch protection, required checks, signed commits — applied with one command.
- [Releasing projects](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/releasing-projects.md): the reusable release pipeline any managed project opts into — tag, build, SBOM/provenance, publish.
- [Release pipeline setup](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/release.md): `koryph release setup` — the caller workflow, release-please config, and manifest it installs.
- [Release bot](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/release-bot.md): provisioning the GitHub App bot identity with `koryph bot` (GitLab: access-token flow).
- [Supply-chain verification](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/supply-chain.md): verifying a release's checksums, keyless cosign signature, SLSA provenance, and SBOM.
- [Collaboration](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/collaboration.md): the shared beads/Dolt task graph across collaborators.
- [Intake](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/intake.md): pluggable issue intake — GitHub, Linear, JIRA — into planning beads.
- [CI pipeline setup](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/ci-setup.md): `koryph ci setup` — forge-native CI assets that give koryph its green gate.
- [Epic validation](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/epic-validation.md): the whole-epic review after the last child closes — gap/structural follow-ups, rounds, parking.
- [Observability](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/observability.md): structured logs/traces/metrics under `~/.koryph/telemetry/`; optional OTLP export.
- [Doctor](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/doctor.md): `koryph doctor` — config validation, zombie-lease and orphan-worktree detection, `--fix`.
- [Data retention & gc](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/gc.md): `koryph gc` — the retention policy, `retention.json`, the `never` sentinel, opt-in auto-gc.
- [Terminal cockpit (TUI)](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/tui.md): `koryph tui` — watch and lightly operate running projects from any terminal.
- [VS Code extension](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/user-guide/vscode-extension.md): agent-wave visibility and steering inside the editor — threads, transcripts, quota burn.
- [IDE integration](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/ide-integration.md): editor/account isolation and IDE setup.

## Developer guide

- [Architecture](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): system overview and Mermaid diagrams of the wave loop and data flow.
- [Packages](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/packages.md): the `internal/*` package map (engine, dispatch, merge, sched, quota, govern, …) and the dispatch-shape / footprint rules.
- [Global governor](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/global-governor.md): the cross-process, daemonless concurrency governor (flock + fair-share leases under `~/.koryph`).
- [Testing](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/testing.md): test layout, fixtures, and the green gate.
- [Releasing & versioning](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/releasing.md): semver, engine-version pinning, keyless cosign.
- [Self-hosting](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/self-hosting.md): koryph builds koryph — what the loop's own data buys during development.
- [Editor setup](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/developer-guide/ide-setup.md): workspace setup for contributors working on koryph itself.

## Reference

- [CLI reference](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/reference/cli.md): every `koryph` command, flag, and environment variable.

## Project

- [Features](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/features.md): every major capability, grouped Adopt → Plan → Build → Gate → Govern → Recover → Operate → Protect → Ship, with links.
- [How koryph compares](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/compare.md): the July-2026 agent-orchestration landscape — Claude Managed Agents, Gas Town, Agenc, Conductor, and the wider field — with a capability matrix.
- [Security](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/security.md): how the project secures its supply chain, how the product protects machines/accounts, and what it deliberately does not claim.
- [Community & contributing](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/docs/community.md): filing issues (forms at https://github.com/koryph/koryph/issues/new/choose), contribution rules, licensing model.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): contributor rules (DCO, signed commits, Conventional Commits, `make gate`).
- [Security policy](https://github.com/koryph/koryph/blob/main/SECURITY.md): private vulnerability reporting via GitHub Security Advisories — never public issues.
